description: Next-session forecasts for all holdings and market indices. Generated before each trading session. Accuracy is tracked session-by-session to build a feedback loop.

next_session.dominant_variable: Trump Iran deadline 8pm ET April 7 (= ~5:30am IST April 8 — BEFORE market open). By the time NSE opens at 9:15 AM IST, the deadline outcome will be KNOWN. This single event determines the entire April 8 session direction. Binary: strikes → gap-down to 22,000-22,500; deal/extension → gap-up to 23,200+.

scenarios.label: Escalation — US Strikes Iran
scenarios.description: Trump acts on deadline. US strikes Iranian power plants, bridges, or infrastructure. Crude spikes to $120-130. Nifty gaps down sharply at open.
scenarios.market_tone: Severe gap-down open. P003 thesis approaching target. INDIGO sell immediately (A002 trigger met). SETFGOLD surges. ASTRA MICRO rallies further on defense demand.

scenarios.label: Extension / Talks Continue
scenarios.description: Trump extends deadline again. Iran's 10-point Pakistan-mediated proposal buys more time. No military action. Market opens with modest gap-down reflecting uncertainty.
scenarios.market_tone: Modest gap-down. War premium lingering. FII selling continues. DII absorbs. Sideways to slightly down session.

scenarios.label: Deal — Iran Accepts Terms
scenarios.description: Iran accepts ceasefire/Hormuz reopening protocol. Crude crashes. Risk-on globally. Nifty gaps up on relief.
scenarios.market_tone: Sharp gap-up rally. INDIGO +5-8%, ADANI PORT +4-6%. SETFGOLD corrects. Bearish predictions P002, P003 at risk of invalidation.

next_session.note: Apr 7 actual outcome: Nifty RALLIED +0.68% despite open fears — market chose 'wait and see'. IT sector drove gains (HCLTECH+2.73%, INFY+2.54%, TCS+2.66%). This time there is no room for 'wait and see' — deadline expires before market opens. Expect higher volatility on Apr 8 vs Apr 7.

key_factors: Iran strike vs extension vs deal
FII selling trend (Apr 7 data not yet available)
IT sector momentum (possible continuation if tech remains bid)
index_forecasts.watch: Check GIFT Nifty at 9:00 AM — direction will be immediately clear based on overnight news. Below 22,500 GIFT = strike scenario active.

key_factors: IEA strategic reserve release possible if strikes occur
index_forecasts.watch: FIRST CHECK at 9:00 AM — Brent sets entire day's P&L direction for portfolio

key_factors: Safe haven bid — escalation = strong rally
Iran escalation = immediate sell trigger (A002 active)
Today closed -1.01% even on a market-up day
holding_forecasts.downside_trigger: US strikes Iran → Brent $120+ → SELL 30-50% immediately (A002 trigger)
holding_forecasts.watch: A002 CRITICAL: If Brent >$115 on Apr 8 open → SELL 30-50% before 10:30 AM. Do not wait.

key_factors: Market selloff risk on Apr 8 gap-down
holding_forecasts.upside_trigger: Morning strength opportunity → A001 SELL at ₹278-285 (if rally from deal scenario)
holding_forecasts.watch: A001 still OPEN. If deal scenario C → gap-up gives A001 sell window at ₹278-285. If escalation → do not sell, wait for recovery.

key_factors: Govt diesel export tax = direct margin headwind
Today MRPL rose +0.22% on broad market rally — may reverse on gap-down
holding_forecasts.upside_trigger: Iran deal → Brent crash → MRPL GRM recovery
holding_forecasts.downside_trigger: Brent spikes to $118-120 → MRPL approaches target range ₹160-170 faster
holding_forecasts.watch: P002 thesis tracking — escalation scenario accelerates to target. Monitor daily.

key_factors: PSU sector holds in volatile markets
₹33 buffer above P005 target (>₹430) — very safe
holding_forecasts.upside_trigger: Broad PSU rally on DII buying
holding_forecasts.watch: P005 thesis very safe. Only review if closes below ₹445 (approaching ₹430 zone).

key_factors: Hormuz blockade = direct traffic volume impact
holding_forecasts.watch: A004: If Nifty closes below 22,200 → consider trimming 30% of ADANI PORT.

key_factors: War premium intact as long as deadline is active
SETFGOLD slightly underperforming gold today (-0.88%) — tracking issue possible
holding_forecasts.watch: Best hedge in portfolio. Hold (A005). Exit only on confirmed Hormuz reopening + gold <$4,500.

key_factors: Defense/aerospace play — Iran war directly benefits
holding_forecasts.upside_trigger: Iran escalation → defense stocks rally further. Approaching ₹1,000.
holding_forecasts.watch: Biggest winner today (+5.61%). Watch ₹1,000 breakout level. If holds, consider partial profit booking.

key_factors: Renewable capex theme intact but near-term macro headwinds
holding_forecasts.watch: Earnings April 16 is the key event. No action until then (A006).

key_factors: Consumer staples — defensive in volatile market
holding_forecasts.watch: Defensive holding. Benefits in escalation scenario as funds rotate to staples.

previous_sessions.notes: First session of FY27. No prior forecast was generated for this session.

Forecast was BEARISH_SIDEWAYS (scenario A, -0.8%). Actual: Nifty +0.68% (BULLISH). Direction call: WRONG. Market chose 'wait and see' on Iran deadline rather than selling. IT sector drove gains on USD strength / global tech rotation. Lesson: War premium was NOT pre-priced before deadline expiry — market waited for confirmed outcome.
previous_sessions.error_analysis: Scenario A (extension/status quo) was closest to outcome but with wrong direction. Market actually rallied on IT buying, not on Iran de-escalation. Lesson: IT/tech sector can decouple from geopolitical risk when USD strengthens and global tech rotates.

session_accuracy_summary.notes: First forecasted session (Apr 7) was WRONG on direction. Forecast: down; Actual: up. Reason: IT sector decoupling from geopolitical risk. Will refine sector-specific vs index forecasting in future sessions.
